entries.position_summary: Russia expresses full support for the IAEA safeguards system as the NPT’s verification mechanism and prioritizes its impartial, depoliticized, and technically sound implementation.
entries.quote: Mr. Chair, The Russian Federation expresses its full support for the IAEA safeguards system, which is the NPT verification mechanism. A priority is to ensure its impartial, depoliticized and technically sound application.

entries.position_summary: The country emphasizes that a trusted, impartial, depoliticized, and technically robust IAEA safeguards system is essential to the NPT and to enabling peaceful nuclear uses. It warns that politicization or technical shortcomings in safeguards would undermine the NPT verification regime and broader non-proliferation efforts.
entries.quote: The IAEA safeguards system, which is pivotal for strengthening the NPT and the peaceful uses of nuclear energy, is one of the key elements of this regime. It is essential that the safeguards system be trusted by all States parties to the Treaty. To secure confidence, it should be impartial, politically unbiased, technically reliable and sound. Failure to meet these criteria, as well as any attempts to politicize the IAEA safeguards can severely impair both the NPT verification regime and the nuclear non-proliferation in general.

entries.position_summary: Russia expresses serious concerns about the IAEA’s State-level safeguards concept and demands a comprehensive, policy-body-approved report detailing parameters, application procedures, and rights/obligations. Until such approval, it views State-level approaches as experimental and reserves the right not to recognize conclusions derived from them.
entries.quote: Russia closely follows the situation around the reform of the IAEA Safeguards System, which continues to cause our serious concerns. We expect the Agency's leadership to present a comprehensive report on the State-level concept clearly stating all the parameters of the reform, the procedure for applying the new approach, the rights and obligations of the IAEA Secretariat and Member States. Until the Secretariat has presented such report and it has been approved by the Agency's policy- making bodies, all Secretariat's State-level approaches to safeguards remain purely experimental, leaving the States the right not to recognize any conclusions prepared using such approaches.

entries.position_summary: The country expresses concern over IAEA safeguards reforms, emphasizing potential impacts on national sovereignty and security. It calls for a comprehensive report on the State-level concept detailing all parameters and insists it be submitted to the Board of Governors for consideration and approval.
entries.quote: The reform of the safeguards system carried out by the IAEA Secretariat requires close attention of the Agency's member States, as it affects, to varying degrees, the sovereignty and security of all countries. We are still expecting the IAEA leadership to present a comprehensive report on the State-level concept clearly stating all the parameters of the reform. It should be presented to the Agency's Board of Governors for consideration and approval.

entries.position_summary: They affirm the value of IAEA Additional Protocols but emphasize that adopting them is a voluntary decision, not a mandatory requirement.
entries.quote: We note the importance of Additional Protocols to IAEA Safeguards Agreements. At the same time, we would like to emphasize that concluding such Additional Protocols remains a voluntary step.

entries.position_summary: Russia voices serious concerns about IAEA safeguards reform, particularly the State-level concept, and calls for a comprehensive report clarifying parameters, application procedures, and the respective rights and obligations of the IAEA Secretariat and Member States.
entries.quote: Russia closely follows the situation around the reform of the IAEA safeguards system, which continues to cause our serious concerns. We are still expecting the IAEA leadership's comprehensive report on the State-level concept clearly stating all the parameters of the reform, the procedure for applying the new approaches, the obligations and rights of the IAEA Secretariat and Member States.

entries.position_summary: They acknowledge the Additional Protocol’s value in strengthening confidence in the peaceful nature of nuclear activities, but stress that adopting the Protocol is a voluntary decision for states, not an obligation.
entries.quote: We note the significance of the Additional Protocol to the IAEA Safeguards Agreement for ensuring confidence in the peaceful nature of all nuclear material in the countries and the countries' nuclear activities. At the same time, it is our firm belief that concluding such additional protocols remains a voluntary step.

entries.position_summary: Russia stresses that the IAEA Safeguards System is central to the non-proliferation regime and must be objective, technically sound, and free from political bias to maintain States Parties’ confidence. It warns that any deviation from these principles undermines the IAEA’s verification mechanism and the non-proliferation regime as a whole.
entries.quote: STATEMENT by the Head of the Delegation of the Russian Federation at the Second Session of the Preparatory Committee for the 11th Review Conference of the Parties to the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons (Cluster II: Non-proliferation and IAEA safeguards) Geneva, 26 July 2024 Mr. Chairman, Nuclear weapons non-proliferation regime is one of the pillars of international peace and security. The IAEA Safeguards System plays a pivotal role in supporting it; it was established in accordance with the IAEA Statute, improved after the conclusion of the NPT and today has become universal. The NPT States Parties' confidence in the IAEA Safeguards System is a key to ensuring resilience of the international nuclear weapons non-proliferation regime in general. This confidence can be secured only if the mechanism the Agency applies to verify the States' compliance with their non-proliferation obligations, is objective, technically sound and politically unbiased. The failure to meet these criteria has a most negative effect on the IAEA verification mechanism, and on the non-proliferation regime as a whole.

entries.position_summary: Russia expresses serious concerns about IAEA safeguards reform, specifically the State-level concept, and calls on the Agency’s leadership to provide a comprehensive report detailing the reform’s parameters, application procedures, and the respective rights and obligations of the Secretariat and Member States.
entries.quote: Russia closely follows the situation around the reform of the IAEA Safeguards System, which continues to cause our serious concerns. We are still expecting the Agency's leadership to present an exhaustive report on the State-level concept clearly stating all the parameters of the reform, the procedure for applying the new approaches, the obligations and rights of the IAEA Secretariat and Member States.

entries.position_summary: The country insists that IAEA verification be limited strictly to measures under each State’s Safeguards Agreement and, where applicable, its Additional Protocol. It calls for uniform, transparent, and objective criteria for determining verification intensity and frequency, and for the IAEA to base safeguards conclusions only on information it can openly defend before policy-making bodies.
entries.quote: We believe that only the verification measures and procedures provided for in the State's Safeguards Agreement and Additional Protocol should be applied to this State (for those States that have signed them). The mechanisms determining the frequency and intensity of verification activities should be equally transparent for all Members without exception. They should also exclude the possibility of subjective assessments and rely on clear, measurable and statistically substantiated models. We emphasize that to make safeguards conclusions the IAEA Secretariat should take into account only the information, the credibility of which it is prepared to stand up for in an open discussion at the session of the Agency's policy-making bodies.

entries.position_summary: Acknowledges the importance of the IAEA Additional Protocol for building confidence in the peaceful nature of nuclear activities, but emphasizes that adopting the Additional Protocol is a voluntary, not mandatory, step.
entries.quote: We note the significance of Additional Protocols to IAEA Safeguards Agreements for ensuring confidence in the peaceful nature of all nuclear material in the country and the country's nuclear activities. At the same time, it is our firm belief that concluding such additional protocols remains a voluntary step.

entries.position_summary: Russia supports strengthening IAEA safeguards and the NPT verification system, noting financial and technical contributions through its national safeguards support programme. It highlights decades of efforts to build the IAEA Department of Safeguards’ conceptual and technical capacity.
entries.quote: Russia supports the IAEA's efforts to ensure resilience and improve the efficiency of the NPT verification mechanism. We have contributed money and expertise to this work, including through our national safeguards support programme. For over 40 years of its history, significant work has been done to strengthen the conceptual framework and build technical capability of the Department of Safeguards of the IAEA Secretariat.

entries.position_summary: The country recognizes the Additional Protocol’s value for verifying the peaceful nature of nuclear materials and activities, while emphasizing that adoption of the AP remains a voluntary measure rather than an obligation.
entries.quote: We recognize the significance of the Additional Protocol to the IAEA Safeguards Agreements for confirming that all nuclear material in the country and the country's nuclear activities are of peaceful nature. At the same time, we would like to remind that concluding such Additional Protocols remains a voluntary step.

entries.position_summary: Russia expresses strong support for IAEA safeguards by funding and providing technical assistance, including environmental sample analysis, training IAEA inspectors, and helping develop verification methods for new reactor types such as mobile reactors.
entries.quote: 4 Russia has contributed money and expertise to ensuring reliability of the IAEA control mechanism, including through its national programme of safeguards support. It includes analysing environmental samples collected by the Agency as part of its inspection activities, in Russian laboratories, the training of IAEA inspectors, and assistance in the development of verification approaches for new types of nuclear plants, including mobile nuclear reactors.

entries.position_summary: Russia underscores the central importance of the IAEA safeguards system as the NPT’s verification mechanism and highlights its role in the system’s creation, signaling strong support for safeguards. This reflects endorsement of robust verification under the Treaty.
entries.quote: Its verification mechanism, the IAEA safeguards system, plays a crucial role within the Treaty. Russia is one of the States that stood behind its creation.

entries.position_summary: The country expresses consistent support for the IAEA’s safeguards system and affirms the Agency’s leading expertise in verifying non-diversion of nuclear material from peaceful purposes to weapons. It underscores confidence in IAEA verification to prevent proliferation.
entries.quote: We have consistently supported the IAEA work in safeguards application and recognize the Agency's highest expertise in verifying that nuclear material for peaceful purposes is not used to create nuclear weapons.

entries.position_summary: The country expresses serious concerns about proposed reforms to the IAEA safeguards system and seeks greater clarity and transparency. It calls on the IAEA Director General to provide a detailed report outlining the reform’s parameters, application procedures, and the respective responsibilities and rights of the Secretariat and States.
entries.quote: We are closely monitoring the situation surrounding the reform of the IAEA safeguards system, with respect to which we still have serious questions. We expect the Director General of the Agency to present a report that will clearly set out all the parameters of the reform, the procedure for applying new approaches, and the responsibilities and rights of the IAEA Secretariat and the States parties.

entries.position_summary: Russia supports the universalization of the IAEA Additional Protocol but insists that its adoption remain voluntary and not imposed on states. It favors encouragement over making the AP a mandatory requirement.
entries.quote: The Russian Federation supports the universalization of the Additional Protocol to the Safeguards Agreement. At the same time, we believe that the conclusion of the Additional Protocol is purely voluntary and should not be imposed.

entries.position_summary: Russia supports strengthening IAEA safeguards, providing long-term financial and technical assistance through its safeguards support program to reinforce the Agency’s safeguards framework.
entries.quote: We continue to assist the IAEA Secretariat in strengthening the safeguards system through financial and technical support by means of the Russian safeguards support program. Over four decades of its existence, extensive work has been carried out to reinforce the conceptual and technical framework of the Agency’s Department of Safeguards.

entries.position_summary: Russia emphasizes implementing Article III(2) and supports the NSG and Zangger Committee as frameworks for non-discriminatory nuclear export controls. It aligns its national export control system with NSG Guidelines (INFCIRC/254) and regularly updates its regulations accordingly.
entries.quote: 6 We attach great importance to the implementation of Article III para 2 of the NPT. We believe that the activities of the Nuclear Suppliers Group (NSG) and the Zangger Committee, which was established as the NPT Exporters' Committee, have demonstrated that there is practical possibility of establishing an agreed non- discriminating procedure of nuclear export controls. Russia arranges its national export control system in accordance with the Guidelines for Nuclear Transfers (INFCIRC/254). Russian legislation, including control lists, has been fully harmonized with this document. Regular updates are made to reflect the relevant changes introduced.

entries.position_summary: The country criticizes AUKUS naval propulsion plans, arguing that Australia's withdrawal of HEU from IAEA safeguards under a naval-use exemption sets a dangerous precedent. It warns this could create a new class of non-nuclear-weapon states holding weapons-grade material outside safeguards, thereby weakening the non-proliferation regime.
entries.quote: Moreover, the AUKUS plans can affect the IAEA safeguards system. The Australia-planned withdrawal of highly enriched uranium from the Agency's safeguards will set a precedent for any such programmes in the future. In fact, we will get another category of formally non-nuclear-weapon States in the NPT - States with significant amounts of weapons-grade nuclear material exempted from safeguards. All of this will inevitably lead to weakening the nuclear non- proliferation regime.

entries.position_summary: Supports IAEA safeguards as essential for verifying NPT compliance but insists they be applied with respect for state sovereignty and without politicization or external pressure, which would undermine the NPT’s credibility.
entries.quote: The safeguards system is intended to verify the compliance of the States Parties to the NPT with their non-proliferation obligations. The safeguards should be applied while respecting the sovereignty of States. Any attempts to use them for settling political scores or exercising external pressure undermine the credibility of the NPT's verification mechanism and affect the Treaty itself adversely.

entries.position_summary: The country is skeptical of the IAEA Secretariat’s State-level safeguards approach, characterizing it as experimental. It asserts that states are entitled not to recognize safeguards findings or conclusions derived from this approach.
entries.quote: To our mind, at the moment, State-level approaches to safeguards implemented by the Secretariat remain purely experimental, leaving the States the right not to recognize any safeguards findings and conclusions derived from such approaches.

entries.position_summary: The country supports establishing a Middle East nuclear-weapon-free zone in line with SSOD I and urges states in the region to refrain from nuclear weapons-related activities and place all nuclear activities under IAEA safeguards pending its creation. It contends that Israel’s failure to meet these prerequisites has obstructed the start of substantive negotiations.
entries.quote: 2. The initiative to establish a nuclear-weapon-free zone (NWFZ) was further confirmed by consensus at the tenth special session of the General Assembly (A/S-10/4) in 1978. Moreover, the States Members of the United Nations, within the framework of the then programme of action on disarmament, proposed that “[p]ending the establishment of such a zone in the region, States of the region should … refrain on a reciprocal basis from producing, acquiring or in any other way possessing nuclear weapons and nuclear explosive devices and from permitting the stationing of nuclear weapons on their territory by any third party, and agree to place all their nuclear activities under International Atomic Energy Agency safeguards”.1 Unfortunately, this key requirement of the General Assembly for the start of substantive negotiations has not yet been met by Israel, which has largely predetermined further difficulties in launching the negotiation process.

entries.position_summary: The country emphasizes that IAEA safeguards are central to verifying NPT compliance and must be objective, technically sound, and politically unbiased. It rejects any politicization or use of safeguards to exert external pressure, arguing this undermines the credibility of the NPT verification system.
entries.quote: The NPT nuclear-weapon States' compliance with their non-proliferation obligations is pivotal for ensuring international peace and security. IAEA Safeguards System plays the key role in verifying such compliance. Credibility of the Agency's control mechanism is a key factor of its resilience. Such credibility is only possible if this mechanism is objective, technically sound and politically unbiased. Any attempts to use IAEA safeguards for settling political scores or exercising external pressure are unacceptable, as they undermine the credibility of the NPT verification mechanism and adversely affect the Treaty itself.

entries.position_summary: The country advocates for impartial, non-politicized, and technically robust IAEA safeguards applied transparently and equally to all states. It insists that verification be limited to measures explicitly provided in states’ safeguards agreements and Additional Protocols, and that IAEA conclusions rely only on information the Agency can defend openly.
entries.quote: We believe that the mechanisms that determine verification frequency and intensity must be equally transparent for all countries without exception. We advocate that States should be subject only to the verification measures and procedures provided for in their safeguards agreements and additional protocols in force. We underline that, in its conclusions regarding the application of safeguards, the Agency's Secretariat should take into account only information the reliability of which it is ready to defend in an open discussion. The IAEA safeguards system must be impartial, politically unbiased, technically reliable and sound. Failure to comply with these criteria would be detrimental not only to the Treaty verification regime but also to the objectives of nuclear non-proliferation in general.

entries.position_summary: Russia emphasizes the importance of implementing Article III(2) and endorses the roles of the NSG and Zangger Committee. It argues these mechanisms demonstrate that agreed, non-discriminatory nuclear export control procedures are feasible to support safeguards.
entries.quote: Madam Chair, Russia attaches great importance to the implementation of Article III para 2 of the NPT. We believe that the activities of the Nuclear Suppliers Group and the Zangger Committee have demonstrated that there is practical possibility of establishing an agreed non-discriminating procedure of nuclear export controls.

entries.position_summary: The country emphasizes that NPT nuclear-weapon States must comply with their non-proliferation obligations and that IAEA safeguards are central to verifying such compliance. It underscores the need for the IAEA’s objectivity and for strict adherence by states and the Secretariat to Safeguards Agreements and Additional Protocols to sustain trust in the verification regime.
entries.quote: Mr. Chair, The NPT nuclear-weapon States' compliance with their non-proliferation obligations is pivotal for ensuring international peace and security. The IAEA safeguards system plays the central role in verifying such compliance. Its objectivity, political impartiality and technical soundness, as well as the States' and the IAEA Secretariat's strict adherence to their rights and obligations under the Safeguards Agreements and their Additional Protocols in force are the main criteria ensuring the States parties' trust in the IAEA verification mechanism.

entries.position_summary: Russia supports strengthening IAEA-led NPT verification and has provided funding to improve its resilience and efficiency.
entries.quote: Russia supports the IAEA's efforts to ensure resilience and improving the efficiency of the NPT verification mechanism. We have contributed funding

entries.position_summary: Russia emphasizes support for IAEA safeguards by analyzing environmental samples in its laboratories, training IAEA inspectors for enrichment and plutonium-using facilities, and collaborating on verification approaches for new types of nuclear plants, including mobile units.
entries.quote: In particular, under this programme Russia assists the IAEA by analyzing environmental samples collected by the Agency as part of its inspection activities, in Russian analytical laboratories. We pay considerable attention to training the IAEA inspectors, including in the conduct of inspections on facilities for isotopic separation of uranium and facilities using plutonium. We perform joint missions to elaborate approaches to verification for new types of nuclear plants, such as mobile

entries.position_summary: The country emphasizes that NPT nuclear-weapon States must comply with their non-proliferation obligations and that the IAEA Safeguards System is essential to verify this. It stresses that the Agency’s credibility—and thus effectiveness—depends on being objective, technically sound, and free from political bias.
entries.quote: Mr. Chair, The NPT nuclear-weapon States' compliance with their non-proliferation obligations is pivotal for ensuring international peace and security. IAEA Safeguards System plays the key role in verifying such compliance. Credibility of the Agency's control mechanism is a key factor of its resilience. Such credibility is only possible if this mechanism is objective, technically sound and politically unbiased.

entries.position_summary: Supports universalization of the IAEA Additional Protocol but emphasizes it should remain voluntary and not be imposed as a mandatory requirement.
entries.quote: 6 universalization of the Additional Protocol, but stresses the voluntary nature and inadmissibility of imposing it as a mandatory measure.

entries.position_summary: The country argues that IAEA safeguards must be applied in a non-discriminatory manner and should not be used to restrict states’ legitimate right to peaceful nuclear technology. It emphasizes protecting access to peaceful uses while implementing safeguards.
entries.quote: We underline that the safeguards should not be a pretext for discrimination against certain countries with regard to the exercise of the right to use nuclear technology for peaceful purposes.

entries.position_summary: The country criticizes a partnership transferring nuclear material likely to be outside comprehensive IAEA safeguards under the naval propulsion exemption. It warns that, even if not violating safeguards agreements, this sets a precedent that could weaken the NPT regime.
entries.quote: In addition, this partnership involving the transfer of material that is not likely to be under comprehensive IAEA safeguards, while not violating the Safeguards Agreement, sets a precedent that could be used by other States in the future. This leads to weakening the NPT regime.

entries.position_summary: The country emphasizes strict adherence to IAEA safeguards and opposes any politicization of their implementation. It argues that noncompliance or politicization undermines both the Agency and the broader nonproliferation regime.
entries.quote: Failure to abide by them, as well as any attempts to politicize the implementation of the safeguards are detrimental to both the Agency and the cause of the nuclear non-proliferation in general.

entries.position_summary: The country stresses that a report on safeguards should be submitted to the IAEA Board of Governors for consideration and approval, and argues that reform of the IAEA safeguards system is essential to NPT verification and relevant to all States Parties. This reflects support for institutional oversight and comprehensive safeguards reform.
entries.quote: We would like to remind that this report should be presented to the Agency's Board of Governors for consideration and approval. We emphasize that the reform of the IAEA safeguards system is an essential matter of the NPT verification, and it concerns, in one way or another, all States parties to the Treaty.

entries.position_summary: The country advocates for staff from the IAEA, OPCW, and the BWC Implementation Support Unit to attend and play a more prominent role in the NPT Conference, promoting cross-regime engagement. This aims to strengthen the NPT review process and leverage IAEA expertise.
entries.quote: We also consider it important that the staff of the IAEA and OPCW Secretariats, as well as the BWC Implementation Support Unit, attend the Conference and participate more prominently in it in the future.

entries.position_summary: Russia emphasizes cooperation with the IAEA on multilateral fuel-cycle approaches and assured supply mechanisms (e.g., INPRO and the International Uranium Enrichment Center with Kazakhstan) to expand access to peaceful nuclear energy while strengthening the NPT’s non-proliferation regime.
entries.quote: member states of the technical innovations and institutional features that ensure the sustainability of nuclear energy systems. 43 countries and the European Commission are involved in the INPRO project. Russia remains one of the leaders in joint work with the IAEA to develop the Concept of assured supplies and multilateral approaches to nuclear fuel cycle services. The concept contributes to the goals of developing and expanding the geography of peaceful uses of nuclear energy. At the same time, it strengthens the nuclear non-proliferation regime, the objective of the NPT. More specifically, the International Uranium Enrichment Center, established jointly with Kazakhstan, continues to operate in Russia.

entries.position_summary: Russia emphasizes strong cooperation with the IAEA and states it provides political, financial, and expert support to the Agency, indicating support for IAEA safeguards and its broader activities, including peaceful uses. It positions itself as a contributor to the Agency’s work.
entries.quote: Russia attaches special importance to cooperation with the IAEA. We provide continuous political, financial and expert support to the Agency's activities.
